bead.title: Routing evidence epic: update current Policy-era scope and child coverage
bead.description:
PROBLEM
The reopened routing evidence epic `fizeau-5931f1f8` is stale. Its historical close rationale used legacy profile wording, while current routing specs describe Policy-era routing and hard pins. The epic needs current tracker evidence and child-bead coverage before it can be closed or left open intentionally.

CURRENT STATE
The parent epic should cover Policy resolution, hard pins, context eligibility, server-instance identity, correlation-ID stickiness, utilization/performance evidence, and legacy `code-*` cleanup. This bead is a tracker/spec-evidence triage task only: it should update the parent notes/custom fields and file any missing child beads with concrete AC, not implement routing behavior directly.

PROPOSED FIX
Audit the existing child beads and code/spec evidence for each parent acceptance surface. Update `fizeau-5931f1f8` to state its current Policy-era interpretation. If coverage is missing, create focused child beads with command-based acceptance and wire dependencies instead of closing the parent by assertion.

IN-SCOPE FILES AND SURFACES
- DDx tracker metadata for `fizeau-5931f1f8`
- Child bead creation/dependency wiring for missing routing surfaces
- Read-only inspection of `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-model-routing.md`, `docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-006-overrides-as-routing-failure-signals.md`, and routing tests

NON-SCOPE
- Implementing missing routing behavior directly in this triage bead
- Rewriting the parent epic into a new design spec
- Closing the parent unless every surface has explicit implemented-and-tested evidence
bead.acceptance:
1. `go test ./internal/routing/... ./agentcli/... -run 'Policy|IncludeByDefault|HardPin|Routing|Correlation|Server' -count=1` passes for the currently implemented routing surfaces, or the worker files child beads for any named surface that lacks implementation/test coverage.
2. `ddx bead show fizeau-5931f1f8 --json` shows notes or custom fields that identify the current Policy-era interpretation and no longer rely on legacy profile wording as the sole close rationale.
3. `ddx bead list --json` shows existing or newly filed child beads covering hard pins, Policy resolution, context eligibility, server-instance identity, correlation-ID stickiness, utilization/performance evidence, and legacy `code-*` cleanup, with dependencies wired where order matters.

events.body: [{"ac":1,"text":"ddx bead show fizeau-5931f1f8 proves notes or custom fields identify the current Policy-era interpretation and no longer rely on stale profile wording as the sole close rationale.","kind":"negative","verifiable":true},{"ac":2,"text":"ddx bead list --json shows child beads, or newly filed child beads, covering all acceptance surfaces named by the parent epic.","kind":"prose","verifiable":false},{"ac":3,"text":"If any surface is missing implementation or tests, this bead files concrete child beads with focused acceptance instead of closing the parent.","kind":"prose","verifiable":false},{"ac":4,"text":"The parent epic remains open until all missing child beads are closed or is closed with explicit evidence that each surface is already implemented and tested.","kind":"prose","verifiable":false}]
